name: UI Architecture: Layered Slot-Based Shells
description: Build scalable, maintainable UI shells using slot composition and micro-frontend patterns.
template:
UI Architecture Pattern:

1. **Slot-Based Composition**: Use named slots for layout regions instead of prop drilling
   - Header, Navigation, Sidebar, Main, Footer slots
   - Allows parent shells to compose without touching child markup
   - Reference: Web Components Shadow DOM slots

2. **Module Federation Pattern**: For monorepos and micro-frontends
   - Use Webpack Module Federation or Turbopack federation
   - Expose components as remote modules
   - Isolate team deployments
   - Reference: https://webpack.js.org/concepts/module-federation/

3. **Design System Integration**: Ensure component library integration
   - Establish token-based styling (tailwind, design tokens)
   - Maintain single source of truth for design primitives
   - Versioning strategy: semantic versioning, changelogs
   - Reference: shadcn/ui, Chakra UI, Storybook

name: Design Systems: Token-Based Styling & Primitives
description: Create maintainable, scalable design systems with strict tokenization and primitive-first approach.
template:
Design System Best Practices:

1. **Token-First Approach**
   - Define all design tokens as CSS custom properties
   - Use design token frameworks: Style Dictionary, Figma tokens
   - Never hardcode colors or sizes; always reference tokens
   - Example: var(--color-primary-500), var(--spacing-4)
   - Light/dark theme support through token overrides

2. **Primitive-First Components**
   - Start with unstyled, accessible primitives (Button, Input, Card, Stack)
   - Build higher-level components from primitives
   - Ensure all primitives pass accessibility audits (WCAG 2.1 AA minimum)
   - Reference: Headless UI, Radix UI, AriaKit

3. **Documentation & Storybook**
   - Document every component with visual variants, states, accessibility notes
   - Use Storybook as single source of truth for design
   - Include examples of correct/incorrect usage
   - Link to design spec and implementation patterns

4. **Theming Strategy**
   - Support light/dark themes via CSS custom properties
   - Allow runtime theme switching without page reload
   - Test visual regressions across theme variants
   - Ensure sufficient color contrast (WCAG AA minimum 4.5:1)

parameters.name: themingStrategy
parameters.description: CSS variables, Tailwind, styled-components, or CSS-in-JS

name: Monorepo & Workspace Management: Turborepo Pattern
description: Structure scalable monorepos with Turborepo, clear boundaries, and efficient incremental builds.
template:
Monorepo Best Practices:

1. **Workspace Structure**
   - apps/: Next.js, SPA, other executable apps
   - packages/: Shared libraries (ui, utils, hooks, config)
   - tools/: Scripts, CLI tools, code generators
   - docs/: Documentation, guides, architecture decisions

2. **Dependency Management**
   - Use pnpm workspaces for hoisting and disk efficiency
   - Enforce strict dependency boundaries (no circular deps)
   - Version strategy: consistent or independent versioning
   - Document public APIs and breaking changes

3. **Build & Cache Strategy**
   - Use Turborepo for incremental builds and remote caching
   - Cache artifacts (dist, .next, build outputs)
   - Use hash-based task invalidation
   - Reference: https://turbo.build/

4. **Testing Strategy**
   - Run tests in parallel across workspaces
   - Use git diff to run only affected tests (>80% coverage minimum)
   - Isolate test databases per workspace

5. **CI/CD Integration**
   - Deploy only changed packages
   - Run nx affected to detect impact zone
   - Use semantic versioning with automatic changesets

name: AI-Augmented Engineering: LLM Integration & Prompt Patterns
description: Integrate LLMs (Claude, Ollama) into development workflows for code generation and code review assistance.
template:
AI-Augmented Development:

1. **Code Generation with AI**
   - Use Claude/GPT for component scaffolding, tests, and refactoring
   - Version control generated code; don't treat as disposable
   - Validate AI-generated code through code review and testing
   - Document AI decision points

2. **LLM API Integration Patterns**
   - Use streaming for better UX (Server-Sent Events, WebSocket)
   - Implement token budgeting and cost tracking
   - Cache embeddings and model outputs (Redis, Supabase pgvector)
   - Reference: Anthropic Claude API, LangChain

3. **Prompt Engineering for Consistency**
   - Create system prompts for role-based tasks (architect, code reviewer)
   - Use structured output (JSON) for machine-readable responses
   - Build prompt templates with variable interpolation
   - Store prompts in version control

4. **Generative UI Patterns**
   - Use AI to generate form schemas, tables, and layouts from data
   - Keep AI-generated UI within design system boundaries
   - Human review all generative outputs
   - Example: Form.io, MUI Code Generator

name: Enforce: CI/CD Pipelines & Automated Deployment
description: Establish reliable, fast CI/CD with automated testing, linting, and controlled deployments.
template:
CI/CD Best Practices:

1. **Pipeline Stages**
   - Lint: ESLint, Prettier, TypeScript compiler
   - Test: Unit, Integration, E2E tests with >80% coverage
   - Build: Production bundle, SSR pre-render, Docker image
   - Deploy: Staging → Production with health checks

2. **Testing in CI**
   - Run tests in parallel (Jest, Vitest, Playwright)
   - Generate coverage reports
   - Fail on coverage drops below 80%
   - Cache node_modules and build artifacts

3. **Deployment Strategy**
   - Blue-green deployments for zero-downtime
   - Canary deployments for gradual rollout (5% → 25% → 100%)
   - Automatic rollback on health check failures
   - Database migration safety checks before deploy

4. **Monitoring & Alerting**
   - Collect metrics (build time, test duration, deploy success rate)
   - Alert on deployment failures, performance regressions
   - Create incident runbooks
   - Track deployment frequency and lead time for changes

name: Enforce: Team Leadership & Mentoring (12–15 YOE)
description: Leadership practices for Senior Architects managing cross-functional teams and driving architectural decisions.
template:
Team Leadership Standards:

1. **Code Review Governance**
   - Require approval from 2+ architects on critical changes
   - Use CODEOWNERS file to enforce review requirements
   - Document reasoning for architectural decisions in PRs
   - Create domain-specific code review checklists

2. **Mentoring & Knowledge Transfer**
   - Pair less-experienced engineers on critical features
   - Document architectural decisions in ADRs
   - Hold weekly architecture sync meetings
   - Create runbooks for common operational procedures

3. **Agile / Scrum Practices**
   - Break down features into architectural spikes + implementation stories
   - Use story points accounting for design complexity
   - Hold architecture review sessions before sprint planning
   - Track and pay down technical debt systematically

4. **Cross-Functional Communication**
   - Present architecture to product, design, and QA teams
   - Document trade-offs and constraints clearly
   - Schedule design reviews before implementation
   - Use diagrams (C4, sequence, state) to explain complex flows

name: Enforce: UX & Wireframing: User Journey Mapping
description: Design user workflows with clarity, accessibility, and performance in mind.
template:
UX & Wireframing Standards:

1. **User Journey Mapping**
   - Document happy path, error cases, and edge cases
   - Use user flow diagrams (Figma, Miro)
   - Identify friction points and optimization opportunities
   - Test flows with real users before implementation

2. **Wireframing Best Practices**
   - Create low-fidelity wireframes for information architecture
   - Use component-based wireframing (reference design system)
   - Document interaction patterns (hover, focus, loading, error states)
   - Include accessibility requirements in wireframes

3. **Minimalist Design Execution**
   - Follow KISS principle: keep interfaces simple and focused
   - Use whitespace intentionally
   - Reduce cognitive load (avoid feature bloat)
   - Prioritize clarity over decoration

4. **Core User Journey Elements**
   - Entry points (how users get here?)
   - Primary action (what's the main goal?)
   - Secondary paths (alternative flows)
   - Exit points (how do users leave?)
   - Error recovery (what if something goes wrong?)

name: Enforce: Agile / Scrum: Sprint Planning & Ceremonies
description: Establish reliable sprint planning, standups, and retrospectives for team alignment and continuous improvement.
template:
Agile / Scrum Best Practices:

1. **Sprint Planning**
   - Hold 2-hour planning sessions at sprint start
   - Define acceptance criteria for each user story
   - Account for architectural spikes in estimation
   - Create technical subtasks for complex features

2. **Daily Standups**
   - 15-minute daily meetings (async updates for distributed teams)
   - Identify blockers immediately
   - Share progress on tasks
   - Escalate impediments to tech lead

3. **Sprint Review & Retrospective**
   - Demo completed work to stakeholders
   - Collect feedback and create follow-up items
   - Hold retrospectives to identify process improvements
   - Track velocity trend and adjust capacity planning

4. **Backlog Management**
   - Maintain prioritized product backlog
   - Refine backlog items before sprint planning
   - Define definition of done (checklist)
   - Track burn-down and burn-up charts

name: Enforce: RESTful & GraphQL APIs: Schema & Contract Design
description: Design robust, versioned APIs with clear contracts, versioning strategies, and optimal data fetching.
template:
API Design Best Practices:

1. **RESTful API Design**
   - Use standard HTTP verbs (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH)
   - Implement proper status codes (200, 201, 400, 401, 403, 404, 500)
   - Use resource-based URLs (/users/:id, /posts/:id/comments)
   - Support filtering, sorting, pagination with query params
   - API versioning strategy: /api/v1/, /api/v2/

2. **GraphQL Design**
   - Define clear, documented schema with type definitions
   - Implement query complexity analysis to prevent expensive queries
   - Use field-level permissions and rate limiting
   - Support pagination with cursor-based offsets
   - Cache strategies: Apollo Client, Relay Modern

3. **Request/Response Contracts**
   - Document request/response shapes with OpenAPI/GraphQL SDL
   - Validate requests on server using Zod, Yup, or similar
   - Version responses; never remove fields (mark deprecated)
   - Return consistent error response format

4. **Caching & Performance**
   - Implement ETag-based HTTP caching
   - Use CDN for static responses
   - Support compression (gzip, brotli)
   - Cache warming strategies for frequently accessed data
